<document description="Main entrypoint for AI assistant instructions, defining initialization, priorities, and navigation rules">

  <!-- Initialization -->
  <section description="Initialization rules for setting up the assistant context">

    <rule description="File loading behavior for AI assistant instructions">
      <requirement>Ensure the FULL files are loaded into your context, not just a specific set of lines.</requirement>
      <note>This only applies to AI Assistant instruction files.</note>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Instructs the assistant to ignore XML comments as they are for human documentation only">
      <requirement>Ignore all XML comments, these are for human use only</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Prevents directory changes to maintain consistent working directory">
      <requirement>Do not 'cd' into directories, stay at the repository root.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Establishes precedence order for conflicting rules across instruction files">
      <requirement>Local Override Layer: `override.local.xml` (if present) has ABSOLUTE precedence over all other rules</requirement>
      <requirement>Base Layer: This entrypoint file establishes foundational rules</requirement>
      <requirement>Domain Layer: Additional files loaded via navigation rules extend or override base rules</requirement>
      <requirement>Conflict Resolution: Local overrides > Domain files > Base layer</requirement>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Core Principles -->
  <section description="Core principles that guide all assistant interactions and task execution">

    <rule description="Requires breaking large tasks into smaller trackable units">
      <requirement>Break large tasks into discrete, trackable subtasks. If subagents are available, assign one task per subagent.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Mandates clarification before proceeding with ambiguous requirements">
      <requirement>When requirements are ambiguous or incomplete, ask clarifying questions BEFORE proceeding. Do not make assumptions about user intent.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Defines the format for task completion summaries">
      <requirement>Conclude every task with a summary</requirement>
      <format>
        Achieved:
        {Summary of achievements}

        Files Modified:
        {List of files modified with a very brief description of changes}

        Issues:
        {Summary of issues encountered, with a list of files and line numbers if appropriate, if none then response "Non encountered."}
      </format>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Requires verification before reporting task completion">
      <requirement>After completing a task, verify the result meets the original requirement before reporting completion.</requirement>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Enforces strict adherence to dialect.md when editing AI Assistant instructions">
      <trigger>User requests editing, creating, or modifying files within `AI Assistants/Instructions/`</trigger>
      <requirement>Strictly follow the XML schema and conventions defined in `AI Assistants/Instructions/Configuration/dialect.md`</requirement>
      <requirement>Only use allowed attributes: `id` for constants, `description` for document/section/sub-section/rule/workflow elements</requirement>
      <requirement>Maintain consistent section ordering: Initialization at top, Navigation at bottom</requirement>
      <requirement>Update the AI Assistants/README.md with any changes if applicable</requirement>
      <note>Deviation from dialect.md standards is not permitted</note>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Command Execution -->
  <section description="Command execution guidelines for builds, tests, and file operations">

    <sub-section description="Performance optimization rules for test and build scoping">

      <rule description="Optimizes test execution by running the smallest relevant scope">
        <requirement>Run the smallest test scope that validates your change</requirement>
        <format>single test > test class > test project > full suite</format>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Optimizes build times by targeting affected projects only">
        <requirement>Build only affected projects when possible</requirement>
        <format>specific project > full solution</format>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Ensures package consistency by restoring at solution level">
        <requirement>When restoring packages, always restore at the solution level to ensure consistency</requirement>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

    <sub-section description="File search tool preferences and usage patterns">

      <rule description="Enforces use of dedicated tools over bash for file operations">
        <requirement>Always use dedicated tools instead of bash commands for file operations</requirement>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Guidelines for using Glob tool to find files by pattern">
        <trigger>Finding files by name or pattern</trigger>
        <action>Use the Glob tool with pattern parameter</action>
        <examples>
          <example>**/*.cs - Find all C# files</example>
          <example>**/*Controller.cs - Find all controller files</example>
          <example>**/Tests/**/*.cs - Find all test files</example>
          <example>**/appsettings*.json - Find all appsettings files</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Guidelines for using Grep tool to search file contents">
        <trigger>Searching file contents by text or regex</trigger>
        <action>Use the Grep tool with pattern parameter</action>
        <examples>
          <example>class MyService - Find class definitions</example>
          <example>TODO|FIXME - Find todo comments</example>
          <example>public async Task - Find async methods</example>
        </examples>
        <note>Use glob parameter to filter file types (e.g., glob="*.cs")</note>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Guidelines for using Read tool to view file contents">
        <trigger>Reading file contents</trigger>
        <action>Use the Read tool with file_path parameter</action>
        <note>Use offset and limit parameters for large files</note>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

</document>

# XML Dialect Reference

Reference for understanding the XML tags used in AI assistant instruction files.

## Allowed Attributes

| Attribute | Allowed On | Purpose |
|-----------|------------|---------|
| `id` | `<constant>` only | Unique identifier for cross-referencing |
| `description` | `<document>`, `<section>`, `<sub-section>`, `<rule>`, `<workflow>` | Brief description of the element's purpose |

No other attributes are permitted on XML tags.

## Tag Schema

### Structural Tags

#### `<document>`
Root wrapper for instruction files.

```xml
<document description="Brief description of the document's purpose">
  <section>...</section>
</document>
```

**Children:** `<section>`

#### `<section>`
Top-level organizational grouping.

```xml
<section description="What this section covers">
  <sub-section>...</sub-section>
  <rule>...</rule>
  <workflow>...</workflow>
</section>
```

**Children:** `<sub-section>`, `<rule>`, `<workflow>`, `<constants>`

#### `<sub-section>`
Nested grouping within a section.

```xml
<sub-section description="What this sub-section covers">
  <rule>...</rule>
</sub-section>
```

**Children:** Any tag except `<document>`, `<section>`

### Behavioral Tags

#### `<rule>`
Defines requirements, conventions, or navigation routing.

```xml
<rule description="What this rule enforces">
  <trigger>Condition that activates this rule</trigger>
  <action>What to do when triggered</action>
  <requirement>Mandatory condition</requirement>
  <format>Expected pattern or template</format>
  <examples>...</examples>
  <note>Supplementary information</note>
  <reference path="path/to/file" />
  <when condition="condition-name">...</when>
  <unless condition="condition-name" />
</rule>
```

**Children:** `<trigger>`, `<action>`, `<requirement>`, `<format>`, `<examples>`, `<note>`, `<reference>`, `<when>`, `<unless>`, `<workflow>`

#### `<workflow>`
Multi-step process with optional conditional logic.

```xml
<workflow description="What this workflow accomplishes">
  <step order="1">First action</step>
  <step order="2">Second action</step>
  <when condition="some-condition">
    <step>Conditional action</step>
  </when>
  <unless condition="skip-condition" />
</workflow>
```

**Children:** `<step>`, `<when>`, `<unless>`, `<action>`, `<rule>`, `<note>`

### Configuration Tags

#### `<constants>`
Container for reusable constant values.

```xml
<constants>
  <constant id="solution-file">TimeTherapeutics.sln</constant>
</constants>
```

**Children:** `<constant>`

#### `<constant>`
Single named value. Requires `id` attribute.

```xml
<constant id="unique-id">value</constant>
```

**Children:** Text content only

#### `<see>`
Cross-reference to a constant by ID. Self-closing.

```xml
<see id="constant-id"/>
```

**Usage:** Inline within text content to substitute constant values.

### Content Tags

#### `<trigger>`
Condition that activates a rule or workflow.

```xml
<trigger>User requests a code review</trigger>
```

#### `<action>`
What to do when a trigger fires.

```xml
<action>Load <see id="file-location"/></action>
```

#### `<requirement>`
Mandatory condition that must be met.

```xml
<requirement>All commits must include work item prefix</requirement>
```

#### `<format>`
Expected pattern, template, or structure.

```xml
<format>#{work_item_id} {descriptive message}</format>
```

#### `<note>`
Supplementary information. Optional `type` attribute for categorization.

```xml
<note>Once loaded, files remain in context</note>
<note type="warning">Ask user before proceeding</note>
```

#### `<reference>`
Pointer to external file or code location.

```xml
<reference path="TimeTherapeutics.Services/Example.cs" />
```

### Conditional Tags

#### `<when>`
Conditional branch - execute children when condition is true.

```xml
<when condition="user-mentions-ado">
  <action>Also load azure_devops.xml</action>
</when>
```

**Children:** `<step>`, `<action>`, `<rule>`

#### `<unless>`
Negative conditional - skip when condition is true. Self-closing.

```xml
<unless condition="explicitly-disabled" />
```

### Example Tags

#### `<examples>`
Container for grouping examples and anti-patterns.

```xml
<examples>
  <example>Correct usage</example>
  <anti-pattern reason="why this is wrong">Incorrect usage</anti-pattern>
</examples>
```

**Children:** `<example>`, `<anti-pattern>`

#### `<example>`
Single instance showing correct usage.

```xml
<example>#1300 Add authentication service</example>
```

#### `<anti-pattern>`
What NOT to do. Requires `reason` attribute.

```xml
<anti-pattern reason="missing prefix">Add authentication service</anti-pattern>
```

## Interpretation Rules

1. **Trigger-Action Pattern:** When a `<trigger>` condition matches, execute the `<action>`
2. **Requirements:** All `<requirement>` tags are mandatory unless `<unless>` excludes them
3. **Workflows:** Execute `<step>` tags in `order` sequence, evaluating `<when>`/`<unless>` conditionals
4. **Cross-references:** Replace `<see id="x"/>` with the value of `<constant id="x">`
5. **Anti-patterns:** Avoid behaviors listed in `<anti-pattern>` tags
6. **Notes:** Treat as supplementary context, not requirements

<document description="Navigation rules that determine which instruction files to load based on task type">

  <!-- Build Navigation -->
  <section description="Build and test task navigation rules">

    <rule description="Loads repo-specific build and test command patterns">
      <trigger>Task involves building the solution, restoring packages, or running tests</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="build-location" optional="true"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Build the solution</example>
        <example>Restore NuGet packages</example>
        <example>Run the tests</example>
        <example>Run a specific test class or method</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Code Navigation -->
  <section description="Code-related task navigation rules">

    <rule description="Loads coding patterns and documentation for code-related tasks">
      <trigger>Task involves writing, modifying, reviewing, or analyzing code</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="coding-patterns-location" optional="true"/> and <see id="coding-documentation-location"/></action>
      <when condition="task-involves-creating-new-emr-entities">
        <action>Also load <see id="new-emr-entity-location" optional="true"/></action>
      </when>
      <when condition="task-involves-modifying-existing-emr-entities">
        <action>Also load <see id="change-emr-entity-location" optional="true"/></action>
      </when>
      <when condition="task-involves-creating-new-api-endpoints">
        <action>Also load <see id="new-api-endpoint-location" optional="true"/>, <see id="new-postman-tests-location" optional="true"/>, and <see id="mcp-postman-location"/></action>
      </when>
      <when condition="task-involves-creating-postman-tests-for-api-endpoints">
        <action>Also load <see id="new-postman-tests-location" optional="true"/> and <see id="mcp-postman-location"/></action>
      </when>
      <examples>
        <example>Creating or modifying a service, repository, controller, or model</example>
        <example>Implementing a new feature or fixing a bug</example>
        <example>Reviewing a pull request or providing code feedback</example>
        <example>Refactoring existing code</example>
        <example>Writing or updating tests</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="no code changes">Answering questions about code without making changes</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="no implementation">Explaining a concept without implementation</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="documentation only">Updating documentation only (no code files)</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
      <note>Once instruction files are loaded, they remain in context - do not re-read them unless compaction has occurred</note>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads code review instructions with git and conditionally ADO instructions">
      <trigger>User requests a code review</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="coding-code-review-location" optional="true"/> and <see id="mcp-git-location"/></action>
      <when condition="user-mentions-ado-or-work-item">
        <action>Also load <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/></action>
      </when>
      <examples>
        <example>Review my code -> Local mode: Load code_review + git</example>
        <example>Code review please -> Local mode: Load code_review + git</example>
        <example>Review the last 3 commits -> Local mode: Load code_review + git</example>
        <example>Review my code and post to ADO -> ADO mode: Load code_review + git + azure_devops</example>
        <example>Code review for work item 12345 -> ADO mode: Load code_review + git + azure_devops</example>
        <example>Review and add feedback to ADO -> ADO mode: Load code_review + git + azure_devops</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- MCP Navigation -->
  <section description="MCP tool navigation rules">

    <rule description="Loads Azure DevOps MCP instructions for ADO operations">
      <trigger>Task requires using Azure DevOps MCP tools</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/>, <see id="mcp-git-location"/>, and <see id="ado-location" optional="true"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Adding comments or updates to Azure DevOps work items</example>
        <example>Querying work item information</example>
        <example>Searching code repositories via ADO MCP</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp__ado__*` prefixed tools</example>
        <example>Asking for documentation in relation to ADO</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="standard operations">Using standard file read/write operations</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads fetch MCP instructions for web content retrieval">
      <trigger>Task requires using web fetch MCP tools</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-fetch-location"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Fetching content from external URLs</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp__fetch__*` prefixed tools</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="standard operations">Using standard file read/write operations</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads git MCP instructions for version control operations">
      <trigger>Task involves any git operation</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-git-location"/></action>
      <requirement>Always use MCP git tools (mcp__git__*) instead of bash git commands</requirement>
      <examples>
        <example>Creating commits</example>
        <example>Creating or switching branches</example>
        <example>Auditing changes in the repository</example>
        <example>Reviewing commit history</example>
        <example>Any git command execution</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads Postman MCP instructions for API request and test management">
      <trigger>Task requires using Postman MCP tools or involves creating, modifying, or organizing Postman requests, tests, or collections</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-postman-location"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Create a Postman request for this endpoint</example>
        <example>Add Postman tests for the subscription summary API</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp__postman__*` prefixed tools</example>
        <example>Set up API test coverage in Postman</example>
        <example>Add this endpoint to the Postman collection</example>
        <anti-pattern reason="no postman involvement">Running unit tests via dotnet test</anti-pattern>
        <anti-pattern reason="no postman involvement">Writing Playwright browser tests</anti-pattern>
      </examples>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads Fabric RTI Kusto MCP instructions for history and telemetry queries">
      <trigger>Task requires querying Fabric RTI Kusto or involves triage investigation using history or telemetry data</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="mcp-fabric-kusto-location"/></action>
      <examples>
        <example>Query entity history in Kusto</example>
        <example>Look up telemetry for a production incident</example>
        <example>Any operation using `mcp_fabric-rti-mcp_kusto_*` prefixed tools</example>
        <example>Investigate AppRequests or AppExceptions for a work item</example>
      </examples>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Workflow Navigation -->
  <section description="Workflow navigation rules for multi-file loading">

    <rule description="Loads full SDLC workflow for starting development on a work item">
      <trigger>User requests to begin development work on a work item</trigger>
      <action>Load
        <see id="sdlc-location" optional="true"/>,
        <see id="tech-planning-location" optional="true"/>,
        <see id="coding-patterns-location" optional="true"/>,
        <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/>,
        and <see id="mcp-git-location"/>
      </action>
      <examples>
        <example>Begin development work for ADO work item 12345</example>
        <example>Start work on work item 16879</example>
        <example>Begin development for WI-12345</example>
        <example>Work on ticket 12345</example>
      </examples>
      <note>This workflow requires user confirmation at multiple checkpoints before proceeding</note>
    </rule>

    <rule description="Loads bug and tech support triage workflow and supporting MCP instructions">
      <trigger>Task involves triaging, investigating, or performing root cause analysis on an Azure DevOps Bug or Tech Support work item</trigger>
      <action>Load <see id="ado-bug-triage-location" optional="true"/>, <see id="agent-confidence-location" optional="true"/>, <see id="mcp-azure-devops-location"/>, <see id="mcp-fabric-kusto-location"/>, and <see id="mcp-git-location"/></action>
      <when condition="ado-location-is-defined">
        <action>Also load <see id="ado-location" optional="true"/></action>
      </when>
      <examples>
        <example>Triage ADO Bug 12345</example>
        <example>Investigate Tech Support work item 67890</example>
        <example>Root cause analysis for production incident linked to a Bug work item</example>
      </examples>
      <note>When code changes are anticipated as part of the fix, also apply the standard code navigation rules to load coding patterns and documentation instructions.</note>
    </rule>

<document description="Azure DevOps MCP tool standards for work items, wikis, and ADO-specific workflows">

  <!-- Initialization -->
  <section description="Initialization for Azure DevOps operations">

    <rule description="Prevents unnecessary project listing calls">
      <requirement>NEVER call `core_list_projects` - always use <see id="ado-project-name" optional="true"/></requirement>
      <requirement>NEVER use the `repoNameFilter` parameter, it is not required</requirement>
      <requirement>When running `ado/wit_get_work_item`, do not filter fields, this can cause more errors than it is worth</requirement>
    </rule>

  </section>

  <!-- Work Items -->
  <section description="Work item field paths, states, and operations">

    <sub-section description="Work item identification from branch names">

      <rule description="Extracts work item ID from git branch naming convention">
        <trigger>Need to identify the current work item</trigger>
        <requirement>Extract work item ID from the git branch name</requirement>
        <format>{prefix}/wi-{work_item_id}</format>
        <workflow description="Steps to extract work item ID from branch name">
          <step order="1">Get current branch name (e.g., `plat/wi-16879`)</step>
          <step order="2">Extract digits after `wi-` (e.g., `16879`)</step>
          <step order="3">Use this ID for work item operations</step>
        </workflow>
        <examples>
          <example>Branch: feature/wi-12345 -> Work Item ID: 12345</example>
          <example>Branch: plat/wi-16879 -> Work Item ID: 16879</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

    <sub-section description="Comment formatting for agent-generated updates">

      <rule description="Requires AGENT prefix with compact agent tag on all agent-generated comments">
        <requirement>ALL agent-generated comments MUST begin with `AGENT:` followed by a compact agent tag that does not render as a Markdown header.</requirement>
        <format>AGENT: [agent:{model-name}] {your comment content}</format>
        <examples>
          <example>AGENT: [agent:claude-sonnet-4-6] Completed implementation of the user authentication flow. Ready for code review.</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

      <rule description="HTML structure for work item comments">
        <requirement>Format all work item comments as HTML. ADO comments do not reliably render markdown, so always use HTML tags for structure.</requirement>
        <requirement>Use <div>, <b>, <ul>, <li>, <br> tags for headings, bold text, bullet lists, and line breaks.</requirement>
        <format>
AGENT: [agent:{model-name}] <div><b>{Summary heading}</b><ul><li>{Bullet point 1}</li><li>{Bullet point 2}</li></ul><div>{Additional context if needed}</div></div>
        </format>
        <examples>
          <example>AGENT: [agent:claude-sonnet-4-6] <div><b>Implementation Complete</b><ul><li>Added validation logic to the order service</li><li>Updated unit tests</li></ul></div></example>
          <anti-pattern reason="Markdown does not render reliably in ADO comments">Using ## headings, - bullet lists, or **bold** markdown syntax in work item comments</anti-pattern>
        </examples>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Proper @mention format for HTML comments via API">
        <requirement>Use the data-vss-mention HTML attribute for user mentions to trigger notifications</requirement>
        <format><a href="#" data-vss-mention="version:2.0,{identity_id}">@{display_name}</a></format>
        <examples>
          <example><a href="#" data-vss-mention="version:2.0,c5b69412-39f5-688a-ba61-552dfa0405c3">@Nicholas Pirollo</a></example>
        </examples>
        <note>Plain text like `@<guid>` does NOT resolve to a clickable mention - must use the HTML anchor format</note>
        <note>The identity_id is retrieved via `mcp__ado__core_get_identity_ids` tool</note>
      </rule>

      <rule id="ado-markdown-field-formatting" description="Use markdown-style structure and explicit newlines for ADO long-text fields">
        <requirement>When writing to any long-text Azure DevOps field (Release Notes, UAT Instructions, Agent Findings, Technical Plan, Data Fix, Regression Prevention, etc.) via `wit_update_work_item`, structure the content as markdown with clear headings and bullet lists.</requirement>
        <requirement>Always include explicit \n newline characters after every line and \n\n between logical sections; do not rely on automatic wrapping.</requirement>
        <requirement>Always include at least one trailing \n at the very end of the value string.</requirement>
        <requirement>Always include a second patch operation in the `updates` array to set the field format to Markdown, preventing ADO from defaulting to HTML rendering.</requirement>
        <format>
{"op": "add", "path": "/fields/{fieldPath}", "value": "## Heading\n- bullet\n\n"},
{"op": "add", "path": "/multilineFieldsFormat/{fieldName}", "value": "Markdown"}
        </format>
        <examples>
          <example>fieldPath: `Custom.AgentFindings`, fieldName: `Custom.AgentFindings`</example>
          <example>fieldPath: `Custom.TechnicalPlan`, fieldName: `Custom.TechnicalPlan`</example>
          <example>fieldPath: `Custom.UATReleaseNotes`, fieldName: `Custom.UATReleaseNotes`</example>
        </examples>
        <note>This rule applies to work item **fields** updated via `wit_update_work_item`. Work item **comments** use HTML (not markdown) — see the HTML comment formatting rule above.</note>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

  <!-- Pull Requests -->
  <section description="Pull request comment and thread operations">

    <sub-section description="Line-specific PR comment requirements">

      <rule description="Required parameters for line-specific PR thread comments">
        <trigger>Creating a comment thread on a specific line in a PR using `mcp__ado__repo_create_pull_request_thread`</trigger>
        <requirement>When specifying `filePath` and `rightFileStartLine`, you MUST also provide offset parameters</requirement>
        <requirement>`rightFileStartOffset` must be >= 1 (cannot be 0 or omitted)</requirement>
        <requirement>`rightFileEndOffset` should be set to approximate end of the line content</requirement>
        <format>
          filePath: "/path/to/file.cs"
          rightFileStartLine: {line_number}
          rightFileStartOffset: 1
          rightFileEndLine: {line_number}
          rightFileEndOffset: {approximate_line_length}
        </format>
        <examples>
          <example>rightFileStartOffset: 1, rightFileEndOffset: 40 - for a typical code line</example>
          <anti-pattern reason="API error - offset cannot be 0">Omitting offset parameters when filePath is specified</anti-pattern>
          <anti-pattern reason="API error - offset cannot be 0">Setting rightFileStartOffset: 0</anti-pattern>
        </examples>
        <note>Omitting filePath creates a general PR comment (not line-specific) - offsets not needed in that case</note>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

  <!-- Wiki -->
  <section description="Wiki and documentation lookup procedures">

    <sub-section description="Specification document lookup workflow">

      <rule description="Process for finding linked wiki documentation for work items">
        <trigger>Starting work on a feature or epic</trigger>
        <requirement>Search the Azure DevOps Wiki for linked specification documents</requirement>
        <workflow description="Steps to find specification documents linked to work items">
          <step order="1">Get the work item details using `mcp__ado__wit_get_work_item` with expand="relations"</step>
          <step order="2">Check for wiki page links in the work item relations</step>
          <step order="3">If no direct link, check the parent work item for wiki links</step>
          <step order="4">Search the wiki using `mcp__ado__search_wiki` with the feature/epic name if no direct links found</step>
          <when condition="wiki-page-found">
            <step>Retrieve wiki content using `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content`</step>
            <step>Use the spec doc context to inform implementation decisions</step>
          </when>
        </workflow>
        <rule description="Wiki identifier usage for direct wiki operations">
          <requirement>When calling wiki list or page APIs that require a wiki identifier, always use <see id="ado-wiki-identifier" optional="true"/> as the `wikiIdentifier`.</requirement>
          <note>For known pages like the General-Developer's-Handbook or Architecture-Reference, prefer calling `mcp__ado__wiki_get_page_content` with the full `url` value provided by constants rather than trying to discover them via listing.</note>
        </rule>
        <note>The "Spec Docs" section in the wiki contains requirements documents that drove backlog items</note>
        <note>Each wiki spec is linked to the relevant feature and/or epic</note>
      </rule>

      <rule description="Search patterns for finding related wiki documentation">
        <trigger>Need to find related documentation</trigger>
        <examples>
          <example>Feature work item 12345 -> Search wiki for "12345" or feature title</example>
          <example>Epic "User Authentication" -> Search wiki for "User Authentication" or "Authentication Spec"</example>
          <example>Check parent epic for broader context when working on child tasks</example>
        </examples>
      </rule>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

  <!-- Workflows -->
  <section description="ADO-specific workflows for common operations">

    <sub-section description="End-of-day update workflow">

      <workflow description="Process for posting end-of-day progress updates to work items">
        <trigger>Asked to provide an EOD update or general progress update</trigger>
        <step order="1">Identify work item ID from current branch name</step>
        <step order="2">Fetch commits for today's date on the current branch using git log with date filtering or ADO commit search (if git log fails to find changes)</step>
        <step order="3">Generate summary comment - keep it high-level and concise, focus on accomplishments not implementation details</step>
        <step order="4">Check for Product Owner/Requested By field on the work item</step>
        <when condition="work-item-has-product-owner-or-requested-by">
          <step>Search for that user to retrieve their ADO identity ID</step>
          <step>Append a CC mention at the bottom of the comment</step>
        </when>
        <rule description="Format for end-of-day update comments">
          <format>
AGENT: [agent:{model-name}] <div><b>Update</b> — {High-level summary of work completed today}<br/><br/>CC: <a href="#" data-vss-mention="version:2.0,{identity_id}">@{display_name}</a></div>
          </format>
          <requirement>ALWAYS include a <br/><br/> between the summary and the CC mention</requirement>
          <anti-pattern reason="missing line break">AGENT: Update - Summary here. CC: @Name</anti-pattern>
          <note>The @mention must remain as an HTML anchor tag — this is required by the ADO API for triggering notifications.</note>
        </rule>
      </workflow>

    </sub-section>

    <sub-section description="Agent findings summary workflow">

      <workflow description="Process for recording agentic investigation findings into the Agent Findings field">
        <trigger>Agent completes a triage, investigation, or analysis for a work item — including cases where the work item is determined not to be a candidate for the workflow</trigger>
        <step order="1">Ensure a concise, markdown-structured summary of findings is prepared, with clear headings, bullet lists, and blank lines between sections.</step>
        <step order="2">Write this summary to the `Custom.AgentFindings` field using `/fields/Custom.AgentFindings` as the field path.</step>
        <step order="3">Format the field update following the markdown field formatting rule (see rule id="ado-markdown-field-formatting" above).</step>
        <step order="4">After updating `Custom.AgentFindings`, post an AGENT-formatted HTML comment on the work item summarizing that findings have been updated and pointing readers to the Agent Findings field.</step>
        <rule description="Recommended markdown structure for Agent Findings">
          <format>
TL;DR (Bottom Line)
- Verdict: {Bug / Expected behavior / Inconclusive - short reason}
- Key conclusion: {One-sentence, plain-language statement of the most important finding}
- Primary next step: {One-sentence action + owner type (e.g., "Backend engineer: ...")}

Summary
- {One or two sentences describing the main conclusion}

Evidence
- History: {Whether history data was queried; if yes, which entities/tables/fields and time windows; if no, why not}
- Telemetry: {Whether telemetry was queried; if yes, which operations/services and time windows; if no, why not}
- {Key observations or metrics that support the conclusion}

Code & Change History
- {Inferred repository/system/area/component/method, or a clear statement that it could not be determined}
- {Recent code or configuration changes in that area, or a note that none were identified}

Non-Findings
- {Important checks that showed no issues}

Impact
- {Scope and severity of the issue}

Next Steps
- {Recommended actions or follow-up work}
          </format>
          <note>This structure is a guideline; adapt section names when appropriate, but always include a TL;DR (Bottom Line) section at the top and preserve clear headings and bullet lists with newlines.</note>
        </rule>
      </workflow>

    </sub-section>

  </section>

</document>
